zh.lifeSummary: 當William Blake於1360年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾卡爾內時，他的父親Henry Blake25歲，母親Elizabeth Durant30歲。他於1380年與Elizabeth Power在英國英格蘭維爾爾卡爾內結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1461年12月1日在他的家鄉享年101歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格亞特 曼徹斯特甸尼聖 瑪麗 馮 維金。

nameMeanings.name: Blake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (England and central Scotland): variant of Black 1, meaning ‘swarthy’ or ‘dark-haired’, from a byform of the Old English adjective blæc, blac ‘black’, with change of vowel length.

English: nickname from Middle English blak(e) (Old English blāc) ‘wan, pale, white, fair’. In Middle English the two words blac and blāc, with opposite meanings, fell together as Middle English blake. In the absence of independent evidence as to whether the person referred to was dark or fair, it is now impossible to tell which sense was originally meant.
English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English bleik, blaik>, blek(e) (Old Norse bleikr) ‘pale or sallow’ (in complexion).

variants.name: Bleak
meanings: English: mostly a variant of   Blake  , but perhaps also of   Blick  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blaikie
meanings: Scottish: nickname from a pet form of   Blake  .

variants.name: Blakes
meanings: English: variant of Blake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Blake was born in 1360, in Calne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Blake, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth Durant, was 30. He married Elizabeth Power in 1380, in Calne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 1 December 1461, in his hometown, at the age of 101, and was buried in Saint Mary the Virgin, Deane, Greater Manchester, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Saint Mary the Virgin, Deane, Lancashire: 영국 잉글랜드 그리터 먼체스터 디인 세인트 메리 테 버긴
ko.lifeSummary: William Blake(이)가 1360년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 캘니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Blake(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Durant(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 캘니에서 Elizabeth Power(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1461년 12월 1일 그의 고향에서 101세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그리터 먼체스터 디인 세인트 메리 테 버긴에 묻혔다.

Он умер 1 декабря 1461 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 101 год и был похоронен в местности сеинт мэри т виргин, дин, гритир манчестир, Англия, Великобритания.
